behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module handoff boundaries (three collectors -> aggregation/orchestration -> summary input payload). A single-function Unit test is too narrow because the bug can occur when wiring outputs together. Higher levels were considered and rejected: behavior text only requires verifying payload composition before/at summary step, not real HTTP/source infrastructure (so not System) and not model output quality (so not Agentic/Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Wire real collection/aggregation modules together in-process, with deterministic fakes for the three source clients returning distinct sentinel values; execute one report build and assert the exact payload passed into summary generation contains all three source sections/fields.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module error propagation: source client failure must be converted by orchestration/report assembly into a degraded section while continuing the run. Unit is too low because interaction between fetch, error mapping, and renderer is the failure boundary. Higher levels rejected: behavior does not require real OS/network/subprocess (unavailability can be injected by in-process failing stub), and correctness does not depend on model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with two source fakes returning data and one source fake raising a timeout/unavailable exception; assert execution completes, output includes a `data unavailable` marker only for the failed section, and other sections remain populated.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the model's generated content fidelity to input facts; deterministic stubs would hide real misrepresentation/hallucination failures. This is exactly LLM output-quality validation, so Agentic is the minimum. Workflow was considered and rejected because behavior describes a single generation quality check, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real summary-generation model on a fixed dataset with known key values; score output against fact-preservation assertions (e.g., required numeric/value mentions and no contradiction of key metrics) across repeated runs with acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires end-to-end delivery to a real inbox, crossing process/network/email infrastructure boundaries. In-process mocks can pass while hiding SMTP/API auth, formatting, routing, or inbox-delivery failures. Agentic was considered and rejected: text validates delivery outcome (`reaches inbox correctly`), not model decision quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scheduled script (or equivalent entrypoint) against reachable staging/internal endpoints and a real test mailbox; verify one run collects data, generates report, sends email successfully, and the recipient inbox receives the message with expected subject/body/attachment structure.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode spans real infrastructure boundaries: email-service unreachability plus persistence to filesystem and recovery across runs. Mocking can hide real network/connectivity and file persistence issues that only appear across process/run boundaries. Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior is delivery-fallback reliability, not model output decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Force email endpoint unreachability in a system environment (e.g., blocked route or down test service), run the script and verify fallback file is written; then restore email service and execute the next scheduled run, asserting queued report is retried/sent and fallback state is cleared/updated correctly.
